preview_text:
The model favors Turkey at 38%, while the USA is projected to win 31% of the time. Turkey holds a narrow edge over the favored outcome classification, though the separation from the American win probability is modest rather than decisive.

The draw emerges as the modal outcome in the model at 31%, suggesting this match is unlikely to be cleanly decided. The three-way split—no outcome commanding more than 38%—indicates genuine competitive balance. Turkey's slight favorite status and the elevated draw probability point toward a closely contested, potentially open game where all three results carry meaningful likelihood.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully on USA prospects. Bookmakers assign the USA 39% to win, some 8 percentage points above the model's 31%. The market also compresses the draw to 27%, 4 points lower than the model. Turkey sits near consensus at 34% in the books versus 38% in the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these assessments; the discrepancy warrants attention as both sources process the same matchup differently.
